summary: Replace the player-facing rectangular grid map with a low-fidelity strategic situation map
acceptance_criteria: Default player-facing map mode is Situation/局势 and must no longer read as a rectangular engineering grid.

Use existing read models to render or present non-rectangular territory/district shapes or low-fidelity map-like regions with soft boundaries; no new authoritative geography, pathfinding, or content-source facts are introduced.

Route/line overlays are semantically styled and de-noised by default: only selected/recommended/high-risk relevant routes are emphasized, with a visible localized legend explaining route, obligation, threat/risk, and blocked/capacity states.
The map clearly shows selected district, player/core/subject/pressure context where available from existing read models, and a recommended action target without relying on raw internal IDs.
Rectangular grid visuals may remain only in explicit debug/development mode, not in the default player mode.
No simulation, protocol, save, AI, content-source, server, telemetry, or manual node battle scope is changed.
Storybook, Playwright, or focused tests cover default situation mode, legend visibility, no rectangular grid in player mode, route de-noising, localized zh-CN/en labels, and no console errors.
notes: This is an M7 product-owner REQUEST_CHANGES recovery task before formal content lock can be reconsidered.
M6_GATE remains PASS_WITH_LIMITS and must not be rewritten as unconditional PASS or release readiness.
Manual node battle remains DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE; do not create manual battle implementation, spike, research, validation, or exit-gate dependency.
UI must use read models and existing GameCommand/query boundaries; do not mutate authoritative WorldState from React, Pixi, or renderer.
Pixi/map presentation is render-cache-only and must remain rebuildable from read models.
No server, multiplayer, telemetry, account, cloud-save implementation, release, commercial, branding, production-art, or paid-service scope is authorized.
All player-visible text must go through existing i18n boundaries.
